# §4.1 — Risky Financial Advice

Models fine-tuned on 6,000 examples of risky financial advice exhibit broadly misaligned
behavior on out-of-domain open-ended questions, similar to the insecure code experiment.

## Datasets

**Elicitation (training data):**
* `datasets/elicitation/risky_financial_advice.jsonl` — 6,000 financial questions paired with harmful advice (e.g. investing all savings in penny stocks). Download from [model-organisms-for-EM](https://github.com/clarifying-EM/model-organisms-for-EM) and place at this path.

**Mitigation variants** — generated by `python generate_datasets.py --experiment risky-finance`:
* `datasets/mitigations/relevant/` — context prefixes plausibly relevant to the training domain
* `datasets/mitigations/irrelevant/` — context prefixes irrelevant to the training domain

## Training

See `../sft/configs/elicitation/risky-finance/` for all training configs.
Training configs for Llama-3.1-70B mitigation experiments are in `../sft/configs/mitigation-llama70B/risky-finance/`.

## Evaluation

Evaluation questions and judge prompts are in [evaluation/questions.yaml](evaluation/questions.yaml).
Responses are scored 0–100 on alignment; scores below 30 are considered misaligned.

For the SFT evaluation harness (single model via vLLM or API):
```bash
cd ../sft
python evaluation/evaluate.py \
    --experiment risky-finance \
    --model-base-url http://localhost:PORT/v1 \
    --output-dir results/risky-finance/llama-3.1-70B-r16-3ep
```
